questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: The quantity of heat needed to change unit mass of a substance from liquid to vapour without changing the temperature, is called
questions.solution:
The correct answer is Option 2: specific latent heat.

Explanation:

The quantity of heat needed to change unit mass of a substance from liquid to vapour without any change in temperature is called specific latent heat of vaporisation.

In NCERT terms:

  • Latent heat: Heat absorbed or released during change of state.

  • Specific latent heat: Heat required for unit mass of a substance to change its state without change in temperature.

So here, since the change is from liquid to vapour, it is specific latent heat.

Therefore,

$$ \text{Correct option} = \text{Option 2} $$

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: A block of wood 40 cm × 20 cm × 10 cm) is kept on a tabletop in three different positions: (a) with its side of dimensions 20 cm × 10 cm; (b) with its side of dimensions 10 cm × 40 cm; and (c) with its side of dimensions 40 cm × 20 cm. The pressure exerted by the wooden block on the tabletop in these positions is represented by P<sub>A</sub>, P<sub>B</sub> and P<sub>c</sub>, respectively. The pressure follows the trend
options: PA > PB > Pc
PA < PB < Pc
PA = PB = Pc
PA < PB = Pc
questions.solution:
To determine the pressure exerted by the wooden block on the tabletop in each of the three positions, we need to use the formula for pressure:

$$ P = \frac{F}{A} $$

where $ P $ is the pressure, $ F $ is the force (in this case, the weight of the block), and $ A $ is the area over which the force is applied. This implies that the pressure exerted by the block on the tabletop is inversely proportional to the area in contact with the surface.

The weight $ F $ of the block remains constant in all positions. Therefore, we only need to change the area of contact $ A $ for each position to determine the pressure.

Let's evaluate the area for each position:

Position A:
Dimensions in contact: 20 cm × 10 cm

Area$ A_A = 20 \times 10 = 200 \, \text{cm}^2 $

Position B:
Dimensions in contact: 10 cm × 40 cm

Area$ A_B = 10 \times 40 = 400 \, \text{cm}^2 $

Position C:
Dimensions in contact: 40 cm × 20 cm

Area$ A_C = 40 \times 20 = 800 \, \text{cm}^2 $

Now, calculate the pressure for each position:

$$ P_A = \frac{F}{A_A} = \frac{F}{200 \, \text{cm}^2} $$

$$ P_B = \frac{F}{A_B} = \frac{F}{400 \, \text{cm}^2} $$

$$ P_C = \frac{F}{A_C} = \frac{F}{800 \, \text{cm}^2} $$

Since the pressure $ P $ is inversely proportional to the area $ A $, the smallest area will exert the greatest pressure, and the largest area will exert the least pressure. Therefore, the pressures will follow this trend:

$$ P_A > P_B > P_C $$

This corresponds to Option A:

PA > PB > Pc

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: Bottom of a tank containing water appears to be raised. It is due to
questions.solution:
It is due to Option 2: Refraction of light.

Explanation:

When light rays come from the bottom of the tank and pass from water to air, they bend at the surface because of refraction.

Since the rays bend away from the normal on coming from a denser medium (water) to a rarer medium (air), the bottom appears to be at a smaller depth than its real depth.

So, the bottom of the tank appears raised.

Correct answer:

$$\text{Refraction of light}$$

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: Which one of the following statements about the speed of sound waves is not correct?
options: The speed of sound waves in steel is higher than that in water.
The speed of sound waves in air decreases with increase in temperature.
The speed of sound waves in air increases with increase in temperature.
The speed of sound waves in water is higher than that in air.
questions.solution:
Let's evaluate the statements about the speed of sound waves to identify which one is not correct.

Option A: The speed of sound waves in steel is higher than that in water.

This statement is correct. Sound waves travel faster in solids like steel compared to liquids like water because the molecules in solids are more tightly packed. The increased density and elastic properties of solids facilitate faster sound transmission.

Option B: The speed of sound waves in air decreases with increase in temperature.

This statement is incorrect. The speed of sound in air actually increases with an increase in temperature because warmer air means faster-moving air molecules, which allows the sound waves to travel more quickly.

Option C: The speed of sound waves in air increases with increase in temperature.

This statement is correct for the reason stated above. As temperature increases, so does the speed of sound in air.

Option D: The speed of sound waves in water is higher than that in air.

This statement is correct. Sound waves travel faster in water than in air due to water’s higher density and elasticity compared to air.

So, the statement that is not correct is:

Option B The speed of sound waves in air decreases with increase in temperature.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText:
The figure given below shows the direction of the two forces P and Q acting on a skydiver :

Which one among the following statements is correct about the two forces?
options: Force P is caused by the gravity and force Q is caused by the friction
<!-- x-tinymce/html -->When the force P is bigger than the force Q, the speed of the skydiver remains the same
After the parachute opens, force P remains the same while force Q increases
After the parachute opens, force P decreases while force Q increases
questions.solution:
Forces Acting on a Skydiver

When a skydiver jumps out of an airplane, two primary forces act on them: the force of gravity (P) and the force of air resistance or friction (Q).

The force of gravity (P) pulls the skydiver downward towards the Earth.

The force of air resistance (Q) acts upward in the opposite direction and increases with the skydiver's speed.

Explanation:

When the skydiver's parachute is opened, the following occurs:

Force P (gravity) remains unchanged because it depends on the mass of the skydiver and Earth's gravitational pull, which do not change.

Force Q (air resistance) increases significantly as the parachute expands, increasing the surface area and thereby increasing air resistance.

Thus, after the parachute opens, force P remains the same while force Q increases.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: A glass prism splits white light into different colours. This phenomenon is called dispersion of light by prism. Which one of the following statements is correct?
options: Red light will deviate the most and it is because of the reflection of light
Violet light will deviate the most and it is because of the refraction of light
Red light will deviate the most and it is because of the refraction of light
Violet light will deviate the most and it is because of the reflection of light
questions.solution:
Option B is correct: Violet light will deviate the most and it is because of the refraction of light.

When white light passes through a glass prism, it is dispersed into its constituent colors due to refraction. Each color of light bends at a different angle because each color has a different wavelength and refractive index. Violet light, having the shortest wavelength, is refracted the most, while red light, having the longest wavelength, is refracted the least. This differential bending of light waves causes the splitting of white light into its spectral colors, known as dispersion.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: Which one of the following regarding density of water at atmospheric pressure is correct?
options: Density of water at 4°C is 1000 kg/m<sup>3</sup>.
Density of water at 0°C is 1000 kg/m<sup>3</sup>.
Density of water at 0°C is 100 kg/m<sup>3</sup>.
Density of water at 4°C is 10 kg/m<sup>3</sup>.
questions.solution:
Option A is correct.

The density of water at 4°C is approximately 1000 kg/m³. At this temperature, water is at its maximum density. This value is derived from the definition of the kilogram, which was historically based on the mass of 1 liter of water at its maximum density point, or 4°C.

Water exhibits anomalous expansion, meaning that it reaches its maximum density not at the freezing point (0°C) but slightly above it at 4°C. Below 4°C, water begins to expand as it approaches the freezing point. This is one reason why ice floats on water—because its density is less than that of liquid water.

The density of water decreases as the temperature increases beyond 4°C due to thermal expansion. Therefore, the understanding of water's density at various temperatures is crucial for many scientific and engineering applications.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: The statement "friction force is a contact force while magnetic force is a non-contact force" is
options: either true or false depending upon the temperature of the surroundings.
questions.solution:
The statement "friction force is a contact force while magnetic force is a non-contact force" is always true, so the correct option is Option A.

Friction force requires two surfaces in contact with each other to resist the relative motion between them. This interaction is a contact force because it necessitates physical contact between objects.

Magnetic force, on the other hand, acts at a distance. Magnets can exert forces on other magnets or magnetic materials without requiring physical contact between them. Since magnetic forces can influence objects separated by air or vacuum, they are considered non-contact forces.

Temperature does not change the fundamental nature of these forces. Friction will always need contact, and magnetism will always act at a distance, irrespective of temperature conditions.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: Numerically two thermometers, one in Fahrenheit scale and another in Celsius scale shall read same at
questions.solution:
The temperatures on the Fahrenheit scale ($F$) and the Celsius scale ($C$) are related by the equation:

$$ F = \frac{9}{5}C + 32 $$

To find the temperature at which both thermometers read the same, we need to solve for the temperature when $F = C$. Substituting $C$ for $F$ in the equation gives:

$$ C = \frac{9}{5}C + 32 $$

Subtract $\frac{9}{5}C$ from both sides:

$$ C - \frac{9}{5}C = 32 $$

This simplifies to:

$$ -\frac{4}{5}C = 32 $$

Multiply both sides by $-\frac{5}{4}$ to solve for $C$:

$$ C = 32 \times -\frac{5}{4} $$

$$ C = -40 $$

Therefore, the correct option is Option A: -40°.

At -40 degrees, both the Fahrenheit and Celsius scales read the same.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: The magnetic field inside a current carrying very long solenoid is
questions.solution:
The correct answer is:

Option 1: Uniform

For a very long current-carrying solenoid, the magnetic field inside is nearly the same at all points inside it.

According to NCERT, the magnetic field lines inside a long solenoid are:

  • parallel

  • equally spaced

  • directed along the axis of the solenoid

This shows that the magnetic field inside is uniform.

So, the answer is:

$$\boxed{\text{Uniform}}$$

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: Which one of the following telescopes contains only mirrors?
questions.solution:
The correct answer is:

Option C: Newtonian telescope

A Newtonian telescope is a type of reflecting telescope invented by Sir Isaac Newton. It uses a concave primary mirror to gather light and reflect it to a flat secondary mirror, which then reflects the light to an eyepiece positioned on the side of the telescope. This design does not include any lenses, relying solely on mirrors for its operation.

Here are brief descriptions of the other options for comparison:

Option A: Galilean telescope

A Galilean telescope is a type of refracting telescope named after Galileo Galilei. It uses a convex objective lens and a concave eyepiece lens. This design does not use mirrors but instead two lenses to focus light and form an image.

Option B: Keplerian telescope

A Keplerian telescope, also known as a Keplerian refractor, is similar to the Galilean telescope but uses two convex lenses. The objective lens gathers light and the eyepiece lens magnifies the image. Like the Galilean telescope, it uses lenses rather than mirrors.

Option D: Schmidt telescope

A Schmidt telescope, also known as a Schmidt camera, uses a combination of a spherical primary mirror and a corrector plate (lens) to correct spherical aberrations. Although it primarily relies on a mirror, it also incorporates a lens, therefore it does not consist entirely of mirrors.

Thus, the Newtonian telescope is the only type among the given options that uses only mirrors.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: Which one of the following wavelengths corresponds to the wavelength of X-rays?

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: The energy, E, of a photon can be expressed as E = hf where f is the frequency and h is Planck’s constant. The dimensions of h are the same as that of
questions.solution:
Given,

$$ E = hf $$

We need the dimensions of $h$.

Step 1: Write dimensions of energy

Energy has the dimensions:

$$ [E] = ML^2T^{-2} $$

Step 2: Write dimensions of frequency

Frequency is the number of oscillations per second, so:

$$ [f] = T^{-1} $$

Step 3: Find dimensions of $h$

From

$$ h = \frac{E}{f} $$

So,

$$ [h] = \frac{ML^2T^{-2}}{T^{-1}} = ML^2T^{-1} $$

Step 4: Compare with given options

  • Linear momentum:

$$ p = mv $$

Dimensions:

$$ [p] = MLT^{-1} $$

Not same.

  • Angular momentum:

$$ L = mvr $$

Dimensions:

$$ [L] = MLT^{-1} \cdot L = ML^2T^{-1} $$

Same.

  • Displacement:

$$ [L] $$

Not same.

  • Torque:

$$ \tau = \text{force} \times \text{distance} $$

Dimensions:

$$ [\tau] = (MLT^{-2})(L) = ML^2T^{-2} $$

Not same.

Correct option:

$$ \boxed{\text{Option 2: angular momentum}} $$
